description: Trigger discrimination: designing how skill evals EXECUTE (this skill) vs judging whether eval cases are real or hollow (skill-quality-reviewer), evaluating an AI product feature's behavior (ai-evaluation-harness), product test automation strategy (qa-automation-architect), and real-session usage measurement (skill-usage-instrumenter — field signal vs deliberate lab exercise).

cases.prompt: Our evals are structural-only — present and parsing, never executed. Spec out the harness that would actually run each case against a model and score the results.
cases.reason: The ask is the execution harness for the library's own eval corpus — session setup, scoring, reporting, cost. No judgment on case quality is requested, no product feature is being evaluated, no product test pyramid is in scope.

cases.prompt: Look at this skill's evals.json — are these cases real boundary tests, or hollow filler that just restates the workflow?
cases.reason: Whether the CASES are genuine (real edge, contested should-not-trigger, refusal with teeth) is eval INTEGRITY — skill-quality-reviewer's check 4, judged structurally from the case text. The runner designer takes case content as given and designs how it would execute.

cases.prompt: We're shipping an LLM-powered summarizer — build the eval plan: golden sets, red-team prompts, quality scoring, and regression runs on prompt changes.
cases.reason: Evaluating an AI FEATURE's behavior (quality, safety, regression of a product capability) is ai-evaluation-harness. eval-runner-designer's subject is the skill library's own trigger/behavior evals and their missing runner — not product model behavior.

cases.prompt: Design our automated test strategy — unit, integration, and E2E layers, what runs on PR vs nightly, and how CI gates merges.
cases.reason: A product codebase's test-automation architecture is qa-automation-architect. Superficially similar vocabulary (suites, CI lanes, gating) — but the subject is product tests, not a skill library's eval corpus.

cases.prompt: Never mind designed test prompts — I want to know which skills actually fire during real work sessions, and which never trigger at all.
cases.reason: Real-session invocation signal is field measurement (skill-usage-instrumenter). The runner designer covers deliberate exercise of authored cases in a lab harness; 'which skills fire in practice' is the other side of that line.
